patches.find: Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi) was substantial partial rather than totality
patches.replace: Beijing ~75% and Changchun ~70% at moderate partial coverage; Lhasa ~50–60% and Urumqi below 40% at lower partial coverage) was partial rather than totality
patches.rationale: H5 (minor): Differentiate coverage tiers among named off-totality cities rather than lumping all as 'substantial partial'. Beijing/Changchun ~70-75%; Lhasa ~50-60%; Urumqi <40%. Values from NASA 2009 July 22 eclipse path geometry.

patches.find: were not necessarily uniformly on that path — the cited 2013 CJG record does not publish a per-site coverage table we have located in English
patches.replace: were not necessarily uniformly on that path; at least one (Wuhan, squarely on the Yangtze-valley corridor of totality) experienced the full eclipse, against which the κ prediction applies without coverage discount. The 2013 CJG record does not publish a per-site coverage table we have located in English
patches.rationale: H1 (moderate): Name at least one confirmed in-corridor SG site (Wuhan) to establish that the network-wide null includes a full-totality station, not only off-path partial sites. Wuhan is on the Chongqing-Wuhan-Hangzhou-Shanghai totality corridor named two sentences earlier.

patches.find: even at 70% partial coverage, a proportionally-scaled ΔB of 3.5–10.5 nT through κ still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal at the off-totality SG sites, 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor
patches.replace: even at 70% partial coverage, a proportionally-scaled ΔB of 3.5–10.5 nT through κ still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal at the off-totality SG sites, 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor (at 50% coverage that drops to 1.5–4.5 µGal, still 1.5× to 45× above the floor; only sites at coverages below ~30% would fall to the floor, and the campaign's mid-latitude stations at Beijing and Changchun were well above that threshold)
patches.rationale: H2 (moderate): Bracket the 70% anchor with a low-end 50% case to show the refutation holds across a range of off-totality coverages, not just the rhetorically convenient 70%.

patches.find: the network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling, not only at full totality
patches.replace: the network-wide null thus refutes κ-coupling at any plausible coverage scaling (quadratic scaling — 0.7² ≈ 0.49 — produces ΔB 2.5–7.4 nT and Δg ≈ 1.5–4.4 µGal, still well above the floor; the argument fails only at scaling exponents implausibly steeper than the eclipse-magnetic literature reports), not only at full totality
patches.rationale: H3 (minor): Make the 'any plausible coverage scaling' claim auditable by including the quadratic scaling computation from EXP-349 defender_pivots_addressed that was inadvertently omitted from the integrated text.

patches.find: Any coverage threshold consistent with exempting 2009 also exempts much of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble
patches.replace: Any coverage threshold high enough to exempt the off-totality 2009 stations would also exclude a substantial fraction of the 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble
patches.rationale: H4 (minor): Replace the over-confident 'consistent with exempting... also exempts much of' framing (which is not anchored in Kim & Chang's actual observatory-to-path distance distribution) with a defensible hedge: 'high enough... would also exclude a substantial fraction'.
